title: Claude Fable 5 (TESTED): UHM... It's actually not worth it..
summary: AICodeKing reviews Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, noting strong benchmark performance in coding, math, and long-context tasks but arguing it does not represent a major practical improvement over existing models like Opus or GPT-4o for typical workflows. The reviewer highlights dual-use safety concerns around cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry capabilities, along with remaining agent limitations and pricing concerns.
writeup: AICodeKing tests Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5, focusing on coding performance, safety mechanisms, and practical usefulness. The review covers safeguards, agentic workflows, and the models' classifier and fallback routing behavior. The takeaway: Fable 5 scores well on benchmarks but, given its cost and limited real-world gains, the reviewer questions whether it warrants switching from existing models like Opus or GPT-5.5.
